---
label: "Tool Schema Design"
icon: "📝"
---

## Tool Schema Design

JSON Schema is the foundation of every <JargonTip term="tool schema">tool definition</JargonTip>. A well-designed schema tells the LLM exactly what parameters are available, their types, constraints, and when they are required. Poor schema design leads to <JargonTip term="hallucination">hallucinated</JargonTip> parameters, wrong types, and failed tool calls.

<CodeBlock b64="" />

<ExpandableSection title="Schema Design Best Practices" icon="📋" defaultOpen={true}>

1. **Descriptive names**: Use verb_noun format (search_database, get_weather). The LLM uses the name to decide when to call the tool.

2. **Rich descriptions**: Tell the LLM when to use the tool, not just what it does.

3. **Use enums**: Constrain string parameters to valid values to eliminate hallucinated values.

4. **Defaults**: Provide sensible defaults so the LLM does not have to specify every parameter.

5. **Minimal required fields**: Only mark truly essential parameters as required.

</ExpandableSection>

<Quiz question="Why are enum constraints in tool schemas important?" options={["They make the schema longer","They prevent the LLM from hallucinating invalid parameter values","They are required by JSON Schema spec","They improve response speed"]} correctIndex={1} explanation="Enums restrict parameters to a known set of valid values. Without them, the LLM might generate invalid values." />
